metadata.purpose: Plain-language student guide built from scraped FVCC data. Replaces the need to navigate 100+ confusing web pages.

getting_started.title: Getting Started at FVCC — What to Actually Do
getting_started.summary: The website makes this feel like 47 steps. It's really 6.

steps.details: Go to https://slate.fvcc.edu/portal/fvcc_app — it's free, no fee. You can also use https://applymontana.mus.edu if you want. FVCC accepts applications on a rolling basis, but get it in at least a week before the semester starts.
Send your high school transcript or GED. If transferring, send college transcripts too. If you have ACT/SAT scores, have those ready — they might save you from placement tests.

You need math and reading/writing placement UNLESS: you have ACT scores from the last 2 years (math 18+ and English 18+/writing 7+), or you're transferring with college math/English credit. If you need them: register through Student Portal → Student Resources → Advising Portal → Placement. Math is through EdReady (online). Reading/writing is Accuplacer (on campus or remote). You can retest and level up.
steps.pro_tip: For math: after your initial placement, you can study and retest UP TO TWO LEVELS higher through the EdReady Study Path. Deadline is 3rd week of the semester.

steps.details: Call (406) 756-3880 to schedule, or drop in via Zoom M-F 10am-2pm at https://fvcc.zoom.us/j/93114034480. They help you pick your program and register for classes. This is mandatory for new students.

Your advisor will walk you through this the first time. After that, you register yourself through the Student Portal. Pay attention to registration windows — sophomores register first, then returning students, then new students.

steps.action: Pay or set up financial aid
steps.details: Payment is due at registration. If you can't pay in full, a deferred payment plan auto-applies (25% in 4 installments for fall/spring, 33% in 3 for summer). File your FAFSA ASAP at https://studentaid.gov — FVCC's code is 006777. Priority deadline is December 1 for spring, March 1 for fall.
steps.where: Business Office in Blake Hall or online through Student Portal

costs.title: What FVCC Actually Costs
costs.summary: Tuition is per credit. Fees are mandatory and stacked on top. Here's the real math.

montana_resident.who: You live in Montana but NOT Flathead County

out_of_state.who: You don't live in Montana and don't qualify for WUE

wue.who: You live in a western state (WA, OR, ID, WY, ND, SD, NM, AZ, NV, UT, CO, HI, AK, etc.) and applied for WUE

hidden_costs: Lab fees — some classes charge extra ($20-200+) and it's NOT on the tuition schedule
Textbooks — budget $300-600/semester depending on your program
Parking permit — if you drive to campus
Program-specific fees — nursing, welding, culinary, trades all have material/supply costs
Health Center fee only kicks in at 7+ credits

payment_rules.deferred_plan: Auto-applied if you don't pay in full. Fall/Spring: 25% x 4 months. Summer: 33% x 3 months.
consequences_of_not_paying: Transcripts held — you can't transfer anywhere
Sent to Montana Dept of Revenue for collections
They can keep your state tax refund

age_65_plus.note: Flathead County residents 65+ pay $52.71/credit instead of $205.16. Massive discount.

financial_aid.title: Financial Aid — How to Not Pay Full Price
financial_aid.summary: Most students qualify for something. The catch: you have to file the FAFSA every year, and do it early.

fafsa.what: Free Application for Federal Student Aid — the single form that unlocks ALL federal and state money
fafsa.when: Available after October 1. File ASAP. Priority deadlines: December 1 (spring) and March 1 (fall).
what_you_need: FSA ID (create at https://studentaid.gov — both you AND a parent if you're under 24)
Prior-prior year tax info (e.g., 2024 taxes for 2026-2027 school year)
fafsa.pro_tip: File by the priority deadline. Work-study money runs out fast. Late filers get less.

pell_grant.what: Free federal money — you don't pay it back
pell_grant.who_qualifies: Based on financial need (EFC from FAFSA). No bachelor's degree already.
pell_grant.part_time: Yes — even 1 credit qualifies for some Pell

fseog_grant.what: Extra free federal money for the neediest students
fseog_grant.who_qualifies: Must also qualify for Pell. Lowest EFC students.

loans.what: Federal Direct Loans — low interest, you DO pay these back
loans.interest: Set by Congress annually, starts July 1
loans.repayment: 6-month grace period after you stop attending or drop below 6 credits
loans.warning: Loans are debt. Borrow only what you need.

work_study.what: Part-time campus job — earn money while in school
work_study.requirement: Must be enrolled in 6+ credits, maintain 2.0 GPA
work_study.important: Work-study does NOT pay your tuition bill directly. You get a paycheck.
work_study.how_to_get_it: File FAFSA by priority deadline, then fill out Work-study Interest Form, then apply for posted jobs at fvcc.edu/jobs

scholarships.what: Free money from FVCC Foundation and other sources
scholarships.tip: Apply for everything. Many scholarships go unclaimed because nobody applies.

eligibility_rules: Must be a U.S. citizen or eligible non-citizen
Must have high school diploma or GED/HiSET
Can't be in default on previous federal loans
Must be enrolled in a degree or certificate program
Must finish degree within 150% of program length (e.g., 60-credit degree = 90 attempted credits max)

calendar.title: Key Dates — What Actually Matters

spring_2026.fall_registration_opens: April 1 (sophomores) → April 2 (returning) → April 15 (new degree) → May 6 (Running Start/non-degree)

calendar.what_refund_dates_mean: If you drop a class after the refund deadline, you still owe the full amount AND get a W on your transcript. Drop early if you're going to drop.

registration.title: Registration — How It Actually Works
for_new_students: Submit documents and do placement testing
Call (406) 756-3880 to schedule an advising appointment
Your advisor registers you for classes at your first meeting
for_returning_students: Meet with your advisor before registration opens
Register through the Student Portal once your window opens
Sophomores get first pick, then returning students, then new students

registration_windows.explanation: FVCC gives priority registration to students further along. If you're a sophomore, you register before freshmen.

degree_types.title: Degree Types — What They Actually Mean

AAS.purpose: Job-ready career degree. You graduate and go to work.
AAS.transfers: Usually does NOT transfer cleanly to a 4-year school. Some exceptions exist.
AAS.examples: Nursing, Welding, Graphic Design, Criminal Justice, IT

AA.purpose: Transfer degree. First 2 years of a bachelor's in liberal arts/humanities/social sciences.
AA.transfers: YES — designed to transfer to Montana 4-year schools
AA.core_complete: If you complete the Montana University System general education core, those credits are guaranteed to transfer.

AS.purpose: Transfer degree. First 2 years of a bachelor's in science/engineering/math.
AS.transfers: YES — designed to transfer to Montana 4-year schools

ASN.purpose: Become a Registered Nurse (RN). You can work after passing NCLEX.
ASN.note: The nursing program is competitive admission — you apply separately after completing prerequisites.

CAS.purpose: Focused career certificate. Shorter than an AAS but still meaningful.

CTS.purpose: Short technical certificate for a specific skill.

degree_types.transfer_vs_career: If you want to transfer to a 4-year school → AA or AS. If you want a job now → AAS or CAS. If you want a quick credential → CTS.

support_services.title: Where to Get Help
support_services.summary: FVCC actually has good support services. The problem is nobody knows they exist because they're buried across the website.

academic_advising.what: Help choosing classes, planning your program, figuring out what to take next

trio_support.what: Extra support for first-generation college students, low-income students, or students with disabilities. Includes mentoring, tutoring, and transfer help.

disability_support.what: Accommodations for documented disabilities (extra time, note-taking, accessible formats)
disability_support.tip: Register with DSS early — accommodations aren't retroactive.

health_clinic.cost: Free (covered by your health center fee at 7+ credits)

library.what: Books, study rooms, equipment loans, research help
library.rooms: Reserve study rooms at the library website

testing_center.what: Placement tests, make-up exams, proctored tests

career_services.what: Job searching, resume help, internships, career planning
career_services.internships: FVCC can connect you with local internships and apprenticeships

veterans_center.what: VA benefits processing, veteran-specific support and community

it_help.what: Login problems, email issues, Canvas/Eagle Online help

website_translation.title: Website-to-English Translation Guide
website_translation.summary: The FVCC website uses a lot of institutional language that means nothing to a normal person. Here's what they actually mean.

translations.Degree-seeking student: You're enrolled in a program that ends with a degree or certificate
translations.Non-degree student: You're taking classes without working toward a specific degree
translations.Satisfactory Academic Progress (SAP): Keep a 2.0 GPA and pass at least 67% of your classes or you lose financial aid
translations.Expected Family Contribution (EFC): How much the government thinks your family can pay. Comes from the FAFSA. Lower = more aid.
translations.Prior-prior year: Two years before the school year. For 2026-2027, they want your 2024 tax info.
translations.Session A / B / C: The semester is split into chunks. A = first half, B = second half, C = the whole thing. Some classes are only 8 weeks.
translations.Running Start: High school students taking college classes. Different tuition rates.
translations.WUE (Western Undergraduate Exchange): Discount tuition for students from western states. About 150% of in-state tuition instead of full out-of-state.
translations.Core Complete: Finishing the general education requirements that Montana's university system guarantees will transfer
translations.Placement testing: Tests to see what level of math and English you start at. Not pass/fail — just placement.
translations.Student Portal: Your online account at FVCC where you register, see grades, check financial aid, and do everything
translations.Eagle Online / Canvas: Where your online classes and course materials live
translations.Credit hour: One credit = roughly one hour of class per week for a semester. A 3-credit class meets about 3 hours/week.
translations.Half-time: 6-11 credits per semester. Matters for financial aid and loan deferment.
translations.Audit: Taking a class without getting a grade. You still pay full price.
translations.W grade: Withdrew from a class. Shows on your transcript but doesn't affect GPA. Still costs you money.
translations.Deferred payment plan: Paying tuition in installments instead of all at once. Automatic if you don't pay at registration.
translations.Hold / flag: Something blocking your account — usually an unpaid balance or missing document
translations.In-service: Faculty training day — campus is closed or has limited hours
